steps.text: Determine if the facility radiation protection program has been maintained in accordance with regulatory requirements and licensee commitments.
Determine if the licensee’s procedures for radiation protection at the facility meet regulatory requirements and licensee commitments.
Determine if Form NRC-3, 'Notice to Employees,' is posted as required.
Determine if restricted areas are posted and controlled as required.
Determine if appropriate protective clothing is used by facility personnel as required.
Determine if the licensee’s personnel dosimetry program is effective, maintained and meets regulatory requirements and licensee commitments.
Determine if licensee personnel, equipment and materials exiting a controlled area are monitored as required.
Determine if personnel working with or around radioactive materials have been instructed in radiation protection as required.
Determine if required radiation surveys, sampling and monitoring were performed in accordance with regulatory requirements and licensee procedures.
Determine if the licensee has implemented a calibration program for the survey, sampling and monitoring instruments in use as required.
Determine if exposure limits in 10 CFR Part 20 were satisfied.
Determine if changes with the potential to affect radiation protection activities were consistent with 10 CFR 50.59, the license and Technical Specification (TS) requirements.
Determine if the licensee addressed radiologically significant events in accordance with regulatory and the licensee’s procedural requirements.
Determine if an ALARA program has been implemented as required.
Determine if the licensee has developed, documented and implemented a radiation protection program in accordance with regulatory requirements.
Determine if the respiratory protection program meets regulatory requirements and licensee commitments.
Determine if planned special exposures were made in accordance with regulatory requirements and licensee procedures.
Determine if the dose to the embryo/fetus for declared pregnant women met regulatory requirements.
Review changes to the facility’s radiation protection procedures.
Ensure Form NRC-3 is conspicuously posted.
Ask the licensee where these forms are posted and directly observe the postings in one or two such areas.
Wear dosimetry as required.
Check alarming dosimeters before each operation.
Assess the need for extremity monitors for handling activated samples and contaminated equipment.
Observe two selected facility workers leaving the controlled area and check if dosimeter readings are recorded as required.
Review the process if the licensee processes their own film or TLD badges.
Conduct an appropriate check of all personnel, tools, equipment, and protective clothing for contamination before leaving a controlled area.
Perform exit monitoring using hand held friskers, stand-in monitors, portal monitors, or hand and foot monitors.
Monitor hands with hand held friskers before removing the probe from its mounting.
Detect alpha contamination if suspected using appropriate methods.
Evaluate the radiation safety training of two permanent facility workers and, where applicable, two experimenters or students.
Observe activities and discuss the radiation safety training received.
Examine surveys of solid waste material to determine whether the licensee complies with their procedures and requirements.
Verify the calibration of radiation monitoring instrument(s) used in the licensee’s radiation surveys.
Review 50 percent of the facility’s personnel dosimetry record.
Determine if there were exposures greater than the 10 CFR Part 20 limits and if the licensee reported the over exposures.
Review major changes in organization, personnel, facilities, equipment, programs, and procedures that could affect radiation protection activities since the last inspection.

preconditions.text: If volatile radioactive materials are used in an area, it is required to be posted and controlled for airborne contamination.
If observation of the protective clothing is not practical, discussions with two individuals to determine their use of protective clothing will provide acceptable samples for inspection.
If individuals routinely exceed 20 percent of the exposure limits, the issue is to be reviewed further to determine that the radiation exposure is consistent with the licensee’s ALARA program.
Changes in the facility or procedures described in the safety analysis report are required to be made in accordance with 10 CFR 50.59.

constraints.text: Radiologically controlled areas are required to be conspicuously posted.
High radiation areas are required to be strictly controlled to prevent unauthorized or inadvertent access.
Requirements for protective clothing may include gloves, booties, rubber boots, hair covers, lab coats, coveralls, and rubber or plastic aprons for use with liquids.
